scaling_analysis: Production vocabulary: 630 unique tokens (BPE-scale). Sequence lengths range 0-100 tokens (mean 48.5). All 5 metrics tested on 2000 pairs with 10000 total checks. Bottlenecks: ROUGE-L has O(mn) LCS computation; BLEU requires n-gram counting up to 4-grams. Circuit compilation cost scales with WFA state count. The system handles production vocabulary without issues at this scale.
honest_assessment: This benchmark uses deterministic pseudo-random pairs from a BPE-like vocabulary of 630 tokens. While production-scale in vocabulary size, the token distribution is uniform rather than Zipfian, and sequences lack grammatical structure. The differential tester verifies that reference, automaton, and circuit implementations agree to within 1e-7 tolerance. 0 disagreements found across 10000 total checks. Real production workloads may exhibit different performance characteristics due to longer sequences, skewed distributions, and multi-language content.
